### The platform behind agentic bioinformatics.

Built and trusted by scientists.

Seqera centralizes your data, orchestrates your pipelines, and keeps everything governed, auditable, and reproducible. Get to insights faster and trust the results when you get there.

[Learn more](https://seqera.io/platform)

### Your Data.  Your Infrastructure.  Your Science.

Integrate Seqera into existing on-premises systems and cloud platforms like AWS, GCP and Azure—with no forced migrations. Your data stays where it needs to stay, your existing AI tools keep working with a secure foundation and without your data going where it shouldn't. Maintain full control over data residency and scale globally, without compromising security or performance.

#### End-to-End Platform.  Built to Scale.

Optimize every workload, from large-scale genomics to advanced AI-based drug discovery. Seqera empowers your teams with a unified environment that delivers high-throughput, cost-effective computation—always auditable and fully monitored.

rnaseq\_testfull\_results s3://1000-genomes-test-data/experiments/0000001/datastudio-vscode-demo s3://1000-genomes-test-data/experiments/0000001/dataCreated bymike-johnson Connected by: 0+0

#### Less Friction.  More Collaboration.  Faster Science.

Seqera connects bioinformaticians, lab scientists, and external partners in one shared environment — where scientists and agentic workflows operate side by side. An intuitive UI, secure APIs, and real-time collaboration built for the way modern R&D actually moves.

#### Comprehensive Compliance. Robust Reproducibility.

Seqera ensures versioned analyses, run tracking, secure execution, and data that stays within your infrastructure. When a submission is due or an audit happens, you have the trail. With built-in controls for access and collaboration, your teams stay aligned, secure, and ready for regulatory demands.

[Get started](https://seqera.eu.auth0.com/authorize?response_type=code&code_challenge_method=S256&code_challenge=8AYGXqqUdRpDfg1uht9iEeFi0ViLtJsagMNLpPioQuQ&client_id=QoEWdPtaRTOZHBmAt6KPu73SCYp9utOL&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fseqera.io%2Fauth%2Fcallback&scope=openid+profile+email+offline_access&state=cHI3SN5pGtSywgGp_gJVJOlXsZSn8IrAbdE_tpKB7f0&nonce=7Ghjkys8qttud1TIKOcI1wh6qvhHlrBEepe0eTcacu0&audience=platform)

Access domain expertise straight from the source. Seqera's dedicated team of bioinformaticians and platform specialists work with you to fine-tune pipelines, troubleshoot issues, and ensure seamless integration with your agents and compute infrastructure.

[Visit Nextflow.io](https://nextflow.io/)

## One Platform for the Scientific Data Lifecycle

Seqera unifies pipelines, data, interactive analysis, AI and compute across the entire scientific data lifecycle, from data ingestion through analysis to results interpretation. Your data never leaves your environment, eliminating migration costs and compliance risks.

Built by the creators of Nextflow, Seqera enables enterprise-grade pipeline orchestration with complete traceability and real-time monitoring. Scale from prototype to production with full data sovereignty and reproducibility at every stage.

![Pipelines](https://seqera.io/_astro/pipeline.BtUq-yFW.gif)

#### Pipelines

Develop, manage, and optimize Nextflow workflows at any scale with comprehensive monitoring, detailed metrics, and resource usage across projects, teams, and organizations.

![Data](https://seqera.io/_astro/data.jbzMdgE3.gif)

#### Data

Unified access to datasets with seamless integration across any cloud storage, enabling faster analysis launch with connected data and end-to-end traceability.

![Interactive analysis](https://seqera.io/_astro/interactiveAnalysis.C81AE6JI.gif)

#### Interactive analysis

Reproducible interactive analysis environments that seamlessly connect to your data and compute. Enable real-time collaboration and data exploration customized to your specific needs.

![AI and ML](https://seqera.io/_astro/AIandML.CT2f8zgQ.gif)

#### AI and ML

Accelerate pipeline development with intelligent code generation and validation; empower scientists to work independently with the latest ML models while maintaining Nextflow best practices.

![Compute](https://seqera.io/_astro/compute.b1pIyY4T.gif)

#### Compute

Optimized, scalable infrastructure that brings compute to your data, with intelligent resource usage and automated environment provisioning to reduce costs and eliminate bottlenecks.

## Built for Bioinformaticians, Trusted by Enterprises

### Deploy Anywhere with Full Data Sovereignty

Complete deployment flexibility across cloud, hybrid, on-prem without forced data migration or vendor lock-in. Data stays in your infrastructure, in your control.

### Seamless Journey from Development to Production

Turn ideas into impact faster. Seqera streamlines the entire workflow lifecycle, enabling you to go from prototype to production without friction or rework.

### Visibility, Flexibility & Control at Every Level

Gain unified insight into every workflow run, dataset, and collaborator. Seqera brings transparency and traceability to your entire operation—no more silos.

## See how Seqera can improve your day-to-day work

From eliminating dependency hell to streamlining collaboration, discover how Seqera solves the biggest friction points in bioinformatics operations.

### Accelerate Development with AI

AI-assisted pipeline development and faster troubleshooting, powered by GitHub Copilot in [Seqera AI](https://seqera.io/ask-ai/chat-v2) and [VS Code](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=nextflow.nextflow&utm_campaign=11293497-Q2-Apr-2025-Product-SeqeraAI&utm_source=website&utm_medium=blog-VScode&utm_content=cta-extension).

### Automate Environment Setup

[Wave](https://seqera.io/wave) containers eliminate time spent configuring tools while [Batch Forge](https://docs.seqera.io/platform-cloud/compute-envs/aws-batch?h=forge#batch-forge) automates enterprise-scale compute environments with full cost control.

### Bring Compute to Data

[Fusion](https://seqera.io/fusion) enables working with massive cloud datasets as local files, eliminating costly egress costs and ensuring secure cross-environment portability.

### Real-Time Pipeline Monitoring

Track pipeline progress, resource usage, and costs in real-time with detailed task-level visibility, automatic failure notifications, and complete audit trails for regulatory compliance.

### Streamline Interactive Analysis

Boost team productivity with cloud-based, interactive development in [Studios](https://seqera.io/studios), giving you seamless access to data, tools, and compute.

### Unified Data Management

Browse and manage datasets across any cloud storage provider or on-prem system from a single interface, with seamless pipeline integration.

### Enterprise-Grade Traceability

Maintain complete audit trails of every pipeline run with full visibility into inputs, parameters, compute environments, and outcomes for regulatory compliance.

### Standardize at Scale

Reproducible containers and automated run environments via Wave and the [Platform API](https://docs.seqera.io/platform-api) or [CLI](https://docs.seqera.io/platform-cloud/cli/overview) enable consistent execution across teams and sites.

### Manage Project Complexity

Built-in governance, versioning, and centralized control, and collaborative workspaces confidently handle project sprawl across the entire organization.

## Flexible Deployment Options

We offer multiple deployment options to meet the needs of pharma and diagnostics teams across the scientific data lifecycle, from R&D to GxP-regulated environments.

web.title: The Seven Bridges Platform
web.description: The Seven Bridges Platform is a cloud-based environment for conducting bioinformatic analyses. It is a central hub for teams to store, analyze, and jointly ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.sevenbridges.com/platform/#content)

# OVERVIEW

The Seven Bridges Platform is a cloud-based environment for conducting bioinformatic analyses. It is a central hub for teams to store, analyze, and jointly interpret their bioinformatic data. The Platform co-locates analysis pipelines alongside the largest genomic datasets to optimize processing. It allocates storage and compute resources on demand, to meet the needs of ever-growing analyses.

The Platform is made for distributed research groups. Since all the tools, data, and compute power live in the cloud or your organization’s data center, you can work with your team, wherever you are.

Collaborate securely by specifying team members’ level of access, including read, write, and execution privileges. Focus discussions around significant genomic regions using the Platform’s commenting system. These resources are all backed up by engineering and research support and a growing, global user community.

Seven Bridges takes the security of your data and results as seriously as you do. Every aspect of our Platform has been designed for work with confidential patient information and complies with all standards governing health records.

[![](https://www.sevenbridges.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/04/iso27001_seal_blue_webversion_150x50px_png.png)](https://cert.schellmanco.com/?certhash=SSBx0FZtsrvd)

Learn more:

[SEVEN BRIDGES SECURITY](https://www.sevenbridges.com/security) [Seven Bridges Compliance](https://www.sevenbridges.com/compliance)

The Platform can operate in a virtual private cloud, a local high performance computing environment, or a hybrid of the two.

The Seven Bridges Platform is the only major biomedical data analysis platform to operate with largest public infrastructure providers— Amazon Web Services, Alibaba, Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud. This means you can store data on one cloud and bring your tools and research questions to the data.

The Seven Bridges Platform is the enabling technology for the use of hybrid clouds—you can build pipelines and analyze data locally, but engage the cloud when you need more powerful computing resources or when your local infrastructure is overtasked. The Platform remains the same, so you can work in a familiar environment across different infrastructures. Moving tools and pipelines between locations is made easy through the Common Workflow Language we have helped to develop.

By elastically scaling the number and types of compute instances – in addition to optimization of pipelines to run in parallel – there are no limits to the number of executions you can run or amount of data you can analyze on the Seven Bridges Platform.

Additionally, elastic scaling also significantly reduces processing time for any given pipeline. For example, the Platform can complete a GATK 2.3 Lite Whole Genome pipeline in approximately five hours – more than twice as fast as a typical execution carried out on a single cloud-based node.

The Seven Bridges Platform hosts a complete library of tools and workflows for bioinformatics. Seven Bridges’ bioinformaticians maintain more than [550 tools and workflows](https://igor.sbgenomics.com/public/apps?__hstc=64521200.58cd487b3503d8a82fc38ea317127f68.1620686872077.1631894926226.1631897991228.109&__hssc=64521200.1.1631897991228&__hsfp=3488606808), ensuring that the latest versions are always available.

Learn more about available [tools](https://www.sevenbridges.com/apps-and-pipelines).

The Platform features a suite of fully customizable visualization tools. It also includes the Seven Bridges interactive [genome browser](http://docs.sevenbridges.com/docs/seven-bridges-genome-browser) built for collaborative investigations.

Research and clinical testing depends on scientists’ ability to replicate results. This is why we made all analyses on the Platform reproducible. Every tool, version, parameter, and piece of data used in an analysis is logged automatically and available for review at a click.

This complete record allows you to track which execution produced a particular output file. It also allows anyone – from remote colleagues to journal editors – to verify your results without the need to distribute code or configure execution environments

The Seven Bridges Platform is fully customizable.

Bring your own software and proprietary tools to the Platform using the Seven Bridges Software Development Kit (SDK). Host your choice of tools and execute them seamlessly alongside other ‘gold-standard’ research tools and pipelines. Use your preferred datasets or sample from the largest library of publicly-available data.

[Learn](https://www.sevenbridges.com/apps-and-pipelines) more about applications on the Platform and how you can add your own.

The Platform is automatable using the Seven Bridges [Application Programming Interface (API)](http://docs.sevenbridges.com/docs/the-api) – upload and manage data; configure and initiate pipeline executions; and explore and download the results.

Integrate the Platform with wider systems and workflows, including LIMS, sequencing machines, analysis packages (e.g., R, Java, Jupyter Lab Notebook, and Python),), and diagnostic reporting services for end-to-end data analysis.

Accurate analysis of biological data is the top priority for Seven Bridges and for all of our customers. Every feature of the Platform works together to ensure that your results are error-free. The Platform uses state-of-the-art bioinformatic tools and implements best-practice pipelines. More still, when tradeoffs between accuracy and speed are required, we always ensure the former. Finally, our Research and Development team works continually to improve the most accurate method of detecting variants: Graph Genome references.

Seven Bridges GRAF™ Suite comprises bioinformatics workflows and tools for secondary analysis of next generation sequencing (NGS) data, based on a pan-genome graph reference to uncover deeper insights into entire populations.

Seven Bridges’ GRAF is a self-improving system for representing and analyzing genomic information using graphs rather than strings of bases. It allows scientists to heavily compress data – and thus operate at unprecedented scale. It is an extremely accurate way to detect individual genetic variation, and this accuracy improves with every new sequence added to the graph. It also offers complete anonymity for individual genomic sequences.

Learn more about Seven Bridges’ [Graph Genome](https://www.sevenbridges.com/graph) suite of tools.

# Seqera Studios: 4 real-world use cases for interactive analysis

[Share "Seqera Studios: 4 real-world use cases for interactive analysis" on Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fseqera.io%2Fblog%2Fstudios-real-world-examples&text=Seqera%20Studios%3A%204%20real-world%20use%20cases%20for%20interactive%20analysis)[Share "Seqera Studios: 4 real-world use cases for interactive analysis" on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fseqera.io%2Fblog%2Fstudios-real-world-examples&title=Seqera%20Studios%3A%204%20real-world%20use%20cases%20for%20interactive%20analysis&summary=Studios%20is%20a%20powerful%20feature%20of%20the%20Seqera%20Platform%20that%20enables%20users%20to%20seamlessly%20transition%20between%20bioinformatics%20pipelines%20and%20interactive%20analysis.%20%20In%20this%20blog%20post%2C%20we%20showcase%20real-world%20applications%20of%20interactive%20analysis%20using%20Studios.%20&source=)[Share "Seqera Studios: 4 real-world use cases for interactive analysis" via Email](mailto:?subject=Seqera%20Studios%3A%204%20real-world%20use%20cases%20for%20interactive%20analysis&body=Hi%2C%20I%20just%20read%20this%20interesting%20article%20on%20Seqera%20Labs%3A%20Seqera%20Studios%3A%204%20real-world%20use%20cases%20for%20interactive%20analysis.%20https%3A%2F%2Fseqera.io%2Fblog%2Fstudios-real-world-examples.)

[Seqera Platform](https://seqera.io/blog/tag-seqera-platform) [Studios](https://seqera.io/blog/tag-studios) [Genomics](https://seqera.io/blog/tag-genomics)

Bioinformatics extends beyond just pipeline runs. Workflows often require additional analysis and human interpretation to deduce meaningful insights for scientific reporting and publication. Interactive environments facilitate this process.

Yet, interactive analysis presents challenges. Analyzing data-in-place is not always possible, resulting in time-consuming data transfers between storage. Bioinformaticians must also navigate multiple programming languages, libraries, and tools while staying within infrastructure limits.

[Learn more about Studios](https://seqera.io/platform/studios/?utm_campaign=7151791-Q1-Feb-2025-Product-StudiosGA&utm_source=website&utm_medium=blog-post&utm_content=mofu-blog-studios-webpage)

## Studios: Closing the loop between pipelines and interactive analysis

[Studios](https://hubs.la/Q03fxjlP0) is a powerful feature of the [Seqera Platform](https://hubs.la/Q038glkd0) that facilitates a seamless transition between bioinformatics pipelines and interactive analysis. Through the creation of on-demand containers, Studios enables you to use familiar tools such as [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) and [R IDE](https://github.com/seqeralabs/r-ide) notebooks, [Visual Studio Code IDEs](https://code.visualstudio.com/), and [Xpra remote desktops](https://xpra.org/) in a dedicated environment customized to your research needs. Each Studio session provides an interactive environment for real-time data analysis and collaboration. In this blog post, we showcase real-world applications using Studios:

1. **Jupyter:** Visualize protein structure prediction data with Python and [py3dmol](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/py3dmol)
2. **R IDE:** Explore RNA-Seq and differential expression data in a custom [Shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/) (R) app
3. **Xpra:** Visualize genetic variants with [Integrative Genomics Viewer (IGV)](https://igv.org/)
4. **VS Code**: Create a portable [Nextflow](https://seqera.io/nextflow/) development environment

> 💡Read the full [Studios for Interactive Analysis guide](https://hubs.la/Q038ggBx0) now

## 1\. Python-based interactive visualization of protein structures in a Jupyter notebook

After running bioinformatics pipelines, researchers often utilize Jupyter notebooks to perform further downstream analysis. With Seqera Studios, this can be achieved without moving your pipeline data. We’ll demonstrate how to visualize predicted protein structures (generated by [Alphafold2](https://github.com/google-deepmind/alphafold) and [ESMFold](https://github.com/facebookresearch/esm) in [nf-core/proteinfold](https://nf-co.re/proteinfold/1.0.0/)) using Biopython and [py3dmol](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/py3dmol) in a Jupyter studio.

> 💡 **Hint:** Studios provides pre-built, version-controlled container template images (for Jupyter, R IDE, Xpra, and VS Code) which are regularly patched with up-to-date packages.

### Create a Jupyter studio

Create or reuse an existing compute environment, mount the pipeline results ( [H1065 sequence](https://nf-co.re/proteinfold/1.1.1/results/proteinfold/results-9bea0dc4ebb26358142afbcab3d7efd962d3a820/) public data from the nf-core/proteinfold test profile, or your own pipeline data), and install any packages and scripts (such as [Biopython](https://biopython.org/) and py3Dmol) needed for interactive protein visualization. [See the Studios guide](https://hubs.la/Q038ggBx0) for detailed instructions and the full Python script.

> 💡 **Hint:** Studios allows you to dynamically build and customize analysis environments with your choice of Conda packages or container templates.

### Visualize protein structures

Use the Jupyter studio to interactively visualize Alphafold2 and ESMFold predicted protein structures of the H1065 sequence in a composite 3D image. [The provided Python script](https://hubs.la/Q038gp9D0) uses a very simple implementation of the [Kabsch algorithm](https://biologicalmodeling.org/coronavirus/accuracy#:~:text=(Bottom%20right)%20The%20Kabsch%20algorithm,to%20notice%20the%20proteins'%20similarity) to compare the Alphafold2 and ESMFold structure predictions.

> 💡 **Hint:** See [Add data using Data Explorer](https://hubs.la/Q038gjQ-0) to add and visualize your own proteinfold data.

Your browser does not support the video.

Video 1: Python-based interactive visualization of protein structures in a Jupyter notebook

## 2\. Downstream analysis of RNA-Seq data and differential expression statistics with R IDE

Combining Studios and R IDE notebooks facilitates seamless interactive analysis using R libraries and tools. We’ll show you how to create a [Shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/) application in an R IDE notebook to explore data from the [nf-core/rnaseq](https://nf-co.re/rnaseq/3.14.0/) and [nf-core/differentialabundance](https://nf-co.re/differentialabundance/1.2.0/) pipelines.

### Create an R IDE notebook studio

After creating or selecting a compute environment and mounting the public nf-core data (or your own pipeline data), launch your interactive studio with the pre-built R IDE container image template. [See the full guide](https://hubs.la/Q038ggBx0) for detailed instructions.

> 💡 **Hint:** See [Add a cloud bucket](https://hubs.la/Q038gjqW0) to use your own pipeline data for interactive analysis.

### Differential expression analysis with RShiny

The RDS data file used in the guide results from using the nf-core/rnaseq pipeline to quantify gene expression in public RNA sequencing data before using nf-core/differentialabundance to derive differential expression statistics. The resulting data is then explored in a custom user interface ( [ShinyNGS](https://github.com/pinin4fjords/shinyngs)) via PCA plots, volcano plots, heatmaps, and tables.

Your browser does not support the video.

Video 2: Downstream analysis of RNA-Seq data and differential expression statistics with RStudio

## 3\. Genetic variant exploration using IGV with Xpra

Spinning up an Xpra remote desktop adjacent to your data can facilitate seamless interactive analysis and pipeline troubleshooting. Here, we use public data from the [1000 Genomes project](http://www.internationalgenome.org/) to perform genetic variant exploration with IGV in an interactive Xpra studio.

### Create an Xpra studio

After selecting a compute environment and mounting the data (the 1000 Genomes S3 bucket), the standardized Xpra container image template can be customized with [IGV](https://igv.org/) and [samtools](http://www.htslib.org/) to suit the needs of this analysis. [See the full guide](https://hubs.la/Q038ggBx0) for detailed instructions on creating an Xpra remote desktop studio.

### View genetic variants in IGV using Xpra

With IGV pre-installed in the Xpra environment, we can launch it and import the [1000 Genomes project](http://www.internationalgenome.org/) data to search for genes of interest, explore exons, and visualize coverage graphs.

> 💡 **Hint:** Studios enables multiple users to connect to the same session, enabling the cross-validation of findings with colleagues in real-time.

Your browser does not support the video.

Video 3: Genetic variant exploration using IGV with Xpra

## 4\. Creating a custom Nextflow development environment with nf-core tools using VS Code

Use Studios with VS Code to build an interactive development environment with all the tools you need to code and run Nextflow. For example, we created an interactive VS Code studio with [Conda](https://anaconda.org/anaconda/conda) and [nf-core tools](https://nf-co.re/docs/nf-core-tools), then executed [nf-core/fetchngs](https://nf-co.re/fetchngs/1.12.0/) before creating a new pipeline with the help of nf-core tools and the Nextflow VS Code plugin.

### Create a VS Code studio

To create a custom VS Code development environment, use the provided VS Code container image, an existing compute environment, and a YAML field to specify Conda, nf-core tools, and other packages to pre-install in your Studio. [See the full guide](https://hubs.la/Q038ggBx0) for detailed instructions.

### Writing Nextflow pipelines in VS Code

Once the VS Code studio has been created, the [nf-core/fetchngs](https://nf-co.re/fetchngs/1.12.0/) pipeline can be run with the Conda profile. You can also create a new Nextflow pipeline with nf-core tools and create a VS Code project for your pipeline in the interactive environment.

Your browser does not support the video.

Video 4: Create a Python Conda environment with nf-core tools to develop Nextflow pipelines

> 💡 **Hint**: The Nextflow [VS Code extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=nextflow.nextflow) makes use of the Nextflow language server for syntax highlighting, code navigation, code completion, and diagnostics in Nextflow scripts and configuration files.

## Why Studios?

[Seqera Studios](https://hubs.la/Q03fxjlP0) lets you seamlessly transition from bioinformatics workflows to secure, interactive analysis environments within your own infrastructure, consolidating data and analytics in one unified location. With built-in templates, you can quickly create flexible, customizable environments by adding packages, libraries, and scripts tailored to your research needs. Additionally, multiple users can connect to the same Studio session, facilitating real-time collaboration within and across organizations.

Interested in finding out more about interactive analysis with Studios? Get started with the full guide below.

[Follow the Studios guide](https://docs.seqera.io/platform-cloud/getting-started/studios?utm_campaign=7151791-Q1-Feb-2025-Product-StudiosGA&utm_source=website&utm_medium=blog-post&utm_content=blog-cta-studios-guide)

## Description

```
Have you discovered a brilliant bioinformatics workflow but you’re not quite sure how to use it? In this webinar we will introduce the power of Galaxy for construction and (re)use of reproducible workflows, whether building workflows from scratch, recreating them from published descriptions and/or extracting from Galaxy histories.

Using an established bioinformatics method, we’ll show you how to:

- Use the workflows creator in Galaxy Australia 

- Build a workflow based on a published method

- Annotate workflows so that you (and others) can understand them 

- Make workflows finable and citable (important and very easy to do!)

Speakers: 

Dr Gareth Price, Head of Computational Biology, QCIF Bioinformatics

Dr Johan Gustafsson, Bioinformatics Engagement Officer, Australian BioCommons

Who the webinar is for:

This webinar is for life scientists and bioinformaticians who need to use and recreate reproducible data analysis workflows. 

This webinar was recorded on 26 October 2022.

The slides from this webinar will be available on Zenodo
https://zenodo.org/communities/australianbiocommons-training

See upcoming webinars and workshops on our website:
https://www.biocommons.org.au/webinars-workshops

Captions are auto-generated.
```
